reasoning: The document contains multiple programmatic major defects, including retained header/footer elements, lists broken by page breaks, incorrect list marker conversion (roman numeral vs alphabetical), and truncated image alt text. Although LLM fidelity composite score is high (0.9701), the presence of 4 major programmatic structural issues requires human intervention.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (6): State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Water Reso; Page 2 of 6; Page 3 of 6
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=6, pages_affected=6/6. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: List broken by page break (2 instance(s)): p3→p4: list item ends “…s within a 29-mile span of the river and”, continues as paragraph; p4→p5: list item ends “…s take appropriate action to require the”, continues as paragraph
concerns.analysis: split_list_count=2. Lists that span page breaks should be extracted as a single continuous structure. Split lists affect reading order and navigability for assistive technology.

concerns.description: Lists converted incorrectly: 1 ordered list(s) rendered as alphabetical (A, B, C) but contain roman numeral markers (I, II, III) — e.g. "II. March 29, 2016 Public Meeting and Oral Comments Summary"
concerns.analysis: roman_letter_confusion=1. The renderer misclassified roman numeral lists as alphabetical starting at letter 'I'. Item text retains original roman numerals alongside incorrect letter markers, breaking list semantics.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 2 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (2 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p1: "... navy blue, with a white evergreen tree silhouette"; p1: "...een on the left, through muted teal in the center,"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=2 (truncated=2, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

The source PDF contains bold and underlined topic names at the beginning of list items summarizing oral and written comments (e.g., 'Air Quality', 'Beneficial Reuse'). The output entirely drops this emphasis, presenting the text as plain text without any markdown or HTML underline tags.
concerns.output_quote: Air Quality – Citizens expressed concerns about fly ash from the sites and the possible health concerns.
concerns.location: Pages 3-5, List items under sections II and III

action_items: Remove retained header/footer text from the main content
Reconnect the list items broken across pages 3-4 and 4-5
Correct the list marker semantics from alphabetical to roman numerals where applicable
Update truncated alt text on images to be fully descriptive
Restore bold and underline visual emphasis on list item topic names if structurally significant
